- What other services Vicky could have under HCP (advised D/A, Allied Health, Equipment & Aids). Family are seeking to have increased P/C & Respite with Podiatry as priority needs for her care plan.

- Discussed fees and that under a HCP 4 - 24 hours available under this budget per fortnight. When advising 2 hourly and hourly rate - noted this is the new financial year rate with travel-to-site fee built into the cost. Advised family were welcome to shop around.
- Discussed ITF and Vicky is a part-pensioner. Advised to complete ITF form (would attach to email with information pack) and await letter back from Services Australia to know if there would be an out of pocket cost.

Family will look at information to consider the HCP and if they wish to take it up or with a different provider. Advised family can request an extension with MAC for her HCP acceptance date to allow more time to decide too.

Good morning,

 

I am reaching out on behalf of a participant who wishes to obtain daily life supports. This is believed to be required as a result of a COVID related MS relapse.

We require the support to commence as soon as possible.

 

Location:

Blackburn

 

Required support:

AM support daily Monday – Sunday

to include, but not limited to – preparation of breakfast, supporting participant to position correctly in bed to have breakfast/drink in bed

Assistance to transfer from bed to shower commode,

Support participant with continence care (bowel) as participant currently experiencing loss of control.  They may elect to use shower commode or toilet (may need to use both).

Fresh linen placed on bed weekly (more frequently if bedding/linen soiled.

Soiled linen or linen/bedding placed into wash as required

Support with self care

Support dry body

Support to dress/grooming as desired

Assist participant into her wheelchair or, if she is ambulating ensure she is safely positioned in recliner

Provision of drink snack – accessible for participant to ensure suitable hydration and food intake until family return home

 

Duration of time:

2.00 hours per shift

 

 

Late afternoon/evening shifts:

Frequency: once per three late afternoons /evenings during the week.

Preference of time/days yet to be confirmed by the participant

Assist with toileting, re-positioning to minimize potential pressure care concerns

If desired, assistance with nighttime attire and preparation for bed

 

Meal Preparation:

Thrice weekly.  At this stage the coordinator is thinking this support could be on alternate afternoons/evenings to scheduled self care support.

However, participant may elect to incorporate this task with afternoon/evening self care support.

 

Female staff only . No age limit , staff must be experienced with self care and assisted/supported transfers which participant will complete.  Present with  Initiative,  be engaging, respectful, calm and discreet are qualities sought.

 

Funds are self-managed – invoices to be directed to the participant.

 

Onboarding/paperwork –

Connect with the support coordinator as they are assisting with this task to ensure supports are placed in a timely manner and to minimize the current MS fatigue levels.

 

Participant has not required such level of support until now, staff will need to be mindful this will initially be a challenge and confronting for the participant to accept.

 

AT/HM/Equipment –

 

Electric lift recliner chair

Hi lo bed with companion bed

Hand held shower rose

Wheelchair with smart assist – waiting on approval of alternate wheelchair

Ramped access to /from the home

Inflatable cushion – in place should participant experience a fall or lower limbs weakness and requires assistance to rise from the ground.

Shower commode – will be hired

4WW

RSL Victoria is a registered charity, member based organisation and is the Victorian Branch of the Returned & Services League of Australia (RSL Australia).

First established in 1916, RSL Victoria’s primary objectives are to provide support to veterans and their families, acknowledge Australian Defence Force service and perpetuate its patriotic duty whilst encouraging conversation and mateship between those who have served and their communities. A crucial role of the RSL is also to appropriately commemorate those who have suffered and died in service of our nation or its allies.

To achieve these objectives, RSL Victoria delivers a range of programs, services and initiatives directly and has 270 Sub-Branches throughout Victoria. Most major towns and suburbs across the state have an RSL Sub-Branch including those who have established significant facilities for local members and their communities.

Each year, throughout the state thousands of dedicated volunteers, over 170,000 members and many more Victorians directly support or participate in achieving the aims of RSL Victoria each year.

ADVENTCARE VILLAGE offers residents a happy place to call home. Why not come and join us?

As well as being just a close walk to the railway station, shops and schools, we are just a stroll from Blackburn Lake.

The personality of the village is created by the residents living here and offers regular bus trips to al l sorts of interesting places, weekly shopping trips,” Cuppa and chat” mornings, exercise classes, speakers and music to entertain us, beautiful gardens all around which are fully maintained by our friendly gardener.

Our ACTIVITY CENTRE is used regularly by the residents, and also by U3A for classes. We hold regular BBQ’s, films showing, and get togethers.

We are covered by 24 hour alarm system, and co-located on site is the residential Aged Care facility., which provides residents with peace of mind knowing That Individualised care is available on sit now and tomorrow.

The independent living units at Regis Inala Village form an exciting combination of communities across an 8.5 hectare site of greenery. Most substantial of all is the wonderful mix of people, diverse cultures and characters.

Inala Village centres around a Village Hub, a bustling place alive with coffee shop conversations of residents and their families, a well stocked supermarket, community chapel and even our own internal bus service.

Our active and enthusiastic residents spend time in countless different ways. From reading in our library areas and relaxing in our tranquil gardens to striding the fairways of local golf courses or strolling around the picturesque Orchard Grove Reserve nearby.

Many indulge their creativity through our craft classes or join social activities like our monthly barbeques, afternoon teas and other special functions such as Chinese New Year. With a weekly bus trip to the local shopping centre you will ensure all your shopping needs are satisfied.

Inala Village also has its own community vegetable garden where residents can grow their own vegetables and herbs whilst also enjoying the company of likeminded gardeners and the fruits of their labour.

With two comprehensive aged care facilities on site – Inala Lodge and Alawarra Lodge, residents have peace of mind that should circumstances change they can move from independent living to low care, through to high care, whilst staying within their own community.

For more information on Inala Village please call our Village Manager on 1800 609 799.
